title: 10 best herbal capsules for cramps

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica capsules helps with cramps because of its natural ability to relax and soothe the muscles. The herb, also known as Dong Quai, has been used for centuries in traditional Chinese medicine to ease menstrual cramps and spasms. Its active compounds, including ligustilide and butylphthalide, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to reduce muscle tension and alleviate cramping pain. By taking angelica capsules, women can experience a significant reduction in discomfort and improve their overall quality of life during their menstrual period.
remedy_properties: Essential oils: Angelica contains a variety of essential oils, including apiol and bergapten, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects that can help relax muscle cramps.
Furanocoumarins: The furanocoumarin compounds in angelica have been shown to inhibit the activity of certain enzymes involved in muscle contractions, helping to reduce muscle spasms and cramping.
Saponins: Angelica saponins have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscles, which can help alleviate menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic glycosides: These compounds, particularly ferulic acid and apiin, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to alleviate cramping by reducing muscle spasms and inflammation.
Alkaloids: Angelica archangelica contains alkaloids such as berberine and magnoflorine, which have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to relax muscles and reduce cramping.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids like angelan and isovaleric acid in Angelica archangelica have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to the relief of muscle cramps by reducing muscle spasms and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part, as they contain the highest concentration of essential oils, including angelic root oil, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and terpenes, which help in relaxing the muscles and relieving menstrual cramps.
Stems: The stems are also used, as they contain a significant amount of essential oils, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, providing relief from cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves in the early morning to ensure maximum potency and freshness.
Clean the harvested roots and leaves by rinsing them gently with cold water for about 10 minutes.
Dry the cleaned roots and leaves using a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 6 hours to remove moisture.
Grind the dried roots and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for about 5 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the powdered angelica archangelica and seal immediately to preserve the herb.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guelder rose capsules helps with cramps because they contain a natural compound called viburnin, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally, viburnin travels to the affected areas of the body, such as the uterus or intestines, and reduces inflammation and muscle spasms that can cause pain. By targeting the root causes of cramping, Guelder rose capsules provide relief from menstrual cramps, as well as cramps related to fibromyalgia, IBS, and other conditions.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Guelder Rose capsules have been found to relax muscle spasms and relieve cramp pain by reducing muscle contractions and increasing blood flow.
Analgesic properties: The herb has analgesic compounds that help reduce pain perception, providing relief from menstrual cramps, headaches, and other types of pain associated with cramping.

Antioxidant properties: Guelder Rose contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of cramps and alleviate symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Viburnin: Viburnin, a glycoside, may help alleviate cramps by exerting a sedative effect on the uterus and smooth muscle, thereby reducing spasms.
Aesculetin: Aesculetin, a coumarin derivative, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that may help to ease cramp-related pain and discomfort.
Viburnine: Viburnine, another glycoside found in Viburnum opulus, has been shown to exhibit a mild sedative effect, which can help to reduce muscle spasms and alleviate cramping.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Viburnum opulus are used to make capsules for cramps due to their high content of salicin and other compounds that help to relax the uterine muscles and ease menstrual cramps.
Leaves: The leaves of Viburnum opulus are used to make capsules for cramps due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relieve cramp-like pain.
Seeds: The seeds of Viburnum opulus are used to make capsules for cramps due to their ability to relax the uterine muscles and reduce menstrual cramp-like pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25g of dried viburnum opulus leaves and flowers from a reliable source to ensure purity.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground plant material with 100g of gelatin or plant-based capsule material in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin or plant-based capsules with 250-500mg of the plant material mixture using a capsule filler.
Seal the capsules using a capsule sealer and store them in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with cramps because of its natural soothing properties that help to relax muscles, ease spasms, and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have a sedative effect on the body, which can help to calm down muscle contractions and alleviate menstrual cramp discomfort. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that contribute to uterine cramps during menstruation. By using herbal chamomile capsules, women can experience a natural and effective relief from menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms associated with cramps.
Relaxant property: Chamomile contains flavonoids like apigenin, which bind to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension that can lead to cramping.
Antispasmodic property: The essential oil of chamomile, particularly bisabolol, has a natural antispasmodic effect, helping to relieve muscle spasms and cramps by relaxing the smooth muscle tissue.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as a muscle relaxant, helping to alleviate cramp pain by reducing muscle spasms and inflammation.
Luteolin: As an anti-inflammatory compound, luteolin helps to reduce cramp pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and chemicals.

Furanoflavonoids (specifically, cis- and trans-isobutyldihydromatricarin and matricarin): These terpenoid compounds have spasmolytic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to relax muscles and reduce cramp pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used due to their high content of apigenin and luteolin, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Seeds: They are often used because they contain a compound called matricarin, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and 500 grams of gelatin capsules size 00.
Mix 100 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 100 grams of dried flowers for 10 minutes.
Add 50 grams of glycerin and 100 grams of distilled water to the mixture and stir for 5 minutes.
Fill each gelatin capsule with 400 milligrams of the mixture and seal the capsules.
Store the herbal matricaria chamomilla capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai capsules helps with cramps because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work to regulate hormonal imbalances in the body. The key active compounds, such as ferulic acid and ligustilide, help to reduce spasms and relax muscles, providing relief from menstrual cramps, backaches, and other types of cramping. Additionally, dong quai capsules have anti-inflammatory properties, which further aid in reducing pain and discomfort associated with cramping.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Dong Quai capsules can relax smooth muscle spasms, reducing the severity and frequency of menstrual cramps, by inhibiting the release of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter that stimulates muscle contractions.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules' anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ferulic acid and coumarin, help reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to menstrual cramp pain and discomfort.

Vasodilatory properties: Dong Quai's vasodilatory effects, attributed to its flavonoids and phenolic acids, can increase blood flow to the pelvic area, helping to relieve cramps by reducing uterine constriction and promoting relaxation of the uterine muscle.
remedy_constituents: Coumarins: These naturally occurring compounds help alleviate cramps by relaxing the uterine muscles, reducing spasms, and improving blood flow to the affected areas.

Phenolic glycosides: These compounds, such as ligustilide and butylphthalide, help reduce muscle spasms and cramps by inhibiting the release of histamine and other pain-causing neurotransmitters, thus providing relief from menstrual cramps.

Terpenoids: Specifically, compounds like ligustilide, have shown analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain associated with cramps by blocking pain signals to the brain and reducing inflammation in the affected areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds that help relax the uterus and alleviate menstrual cramps.
Barks: The barks are used due to their rich content of ferulic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help ease cramp pain.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of essential oils, which have a sedative effect on the uterus, helping to reduce cramp pain and promote relaxation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried angelica sinensis roots from a trusted supplier or cultivate your own.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm place for 2-3 hours or use a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powdered angelica sinensis with 1 part of gelatin or vegetable cellulose in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin or vegetable cellulose capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler for 1 hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with cramps because of their natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relax muscle tissue and reduce spasms. The bioactive compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to inhibit the production of pain-causing hormones, while also improving blood flow and reducing inflammation in the affected area. This targeted relief helps to ease cramping sensations and alleviate discomfort, providing fast and effective relief from menstrual cramps, muscle spasms, and other types of painful contractions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger contains anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce inflammation and ease cramp pain by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause muscle contractions.

Digestive stimulants: The active ingredients in ginger capsules can stimulate digestion and relieve digestive cramps caused by poor digestion or gut motility issues by increasing the movement of food through the intestines.

Pain-relieving compounds: Ginger contains pain-relieving compounds like salicin, which is similar to aspirin, that help reduce pain and discomfort associated with cramps by blocking the production of prostaglandins and reducing inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, are bioactive compounds that help alleviate cramps by inhibiting the production of pain-causing prostaglandins and reducing inflammation in the muscles.
Shogaols: Shogaols, such as 6-shogaol, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which contribute to their ability to relieve cramp pain by reducing muscle spasms and inflammation.

Terpenes (specifically, Sesquiterpenes): Sesquiterpenes present in ginger, such as bisabolol and zingiberene, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to mitigate muscle cramp pain by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected areas.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale contain similar compounds as the rhyzome, making them a suitable alternative for cramp relief.
Buds: The buds are also used due to their high gingerol content, which helps in reducing inflammation and pain associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the rhizomes in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7-10 days at 30-40°c.
Grind the dried rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 1g of the powder with 2g of vegetable cellulose powder to create the capsule filling.
Fill a capsule machine with the powder mixture and seal 200 capsules in about 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with cramps because of their ability to relax smooth muscle tissue. The menthol in peppermint oil has a cooling effect on the muscles, reducing spasms and pain associated with menstrual cramps. Additionally, peppermint's antispasmodic properties help to reduce inflammation and ease cramp severity. By targeting the root cause of the issue, peppermint capsules provide fast and effective relief from cramp discomfort, allowing women to feel more comfortable and focused throughout their day.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Peppermint oil contains anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol and camphor, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate muscle spasms associated with menstrual cramps.
Relaxant properties: The menthol in peppermint oil has a calming effect on the smooth muscles of the uterus, helping to relax and ease uterine contractions that cause cramping and discomfort.
Antispasmodic effects: Peppermint's antispasmodic properties can help to reduce muscle spasms and relieve crampy sensations by relaxing the muscles in the uterus and other areas affected by PMS.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that helps reduce inflammation and relax muscles, thereby alleviating cramp symptoms.
Menthol: A terpene that has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to numb pain and reduce muscle spasms associated with cramps.
Limonene: A monoterpene that has anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic effects, helping to relax smooth muscle and reduce cramp severity.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are commonly used to make capsules for cramps due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to relax uterine muscles and reduce pain.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are used to make capsules for cramps because they contain a high amount of menthol and other volatile oils that help to ease cramping and discomfort.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are used to make capsules for cramps due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relax the uterine muscles, providing relief from cramping and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves at peak potency in the morning.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered mentha x piperita with 500 milligrams of cellulose powder in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the herbal mixture and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover capsules helps with cramps because they contain isoflavones, a type of plant-based estrogen that can help to relax muscles and reduce spasms. These natural compounds have been shown to ease menstrual cramp discomfort by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and subsequent cramping. Additionally, red clover's isoflavones may also help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, further contributing to their anti-cramp effects.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover capsules contain isoflavones, which have been shown to help reduce the severity and frequency of menstrual cramps by relaxing uterine muscle tissue and reducing prostaglandin production.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in red clover capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate cramping symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the uterus, making it easier for the body to eliminate excess prostaglandins.

Phytoestrogens: Phytoestrogens, such as daidzein and formononetin, in red clover capsules mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, which can help regulate menstrual cycles, reduce cramping severity, and promote overall hormone balance.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These plant-based compounds, particularly genistein and formononetin, help with cramps by acting as estrogen mimics, which can relax the uterine muscles and reduce cramping.

Phenolic acids: Compounds like kaempferol and quercetin in Red Clover have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce cramping by minimizing inflammation and oxidative stress in the muscles.

Coumarins: These compounds, including 6-prenylcoumarin and 4'-methoxycoumarin, have a mild estrogenic effect and can help relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping associated with menstrual cycles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in isoflavones, which help to alleviate menstrual cramps by relaxing the uterine muscles.
Seeds: Contain isoflavones, particularly genistein and formononetin, which have a soothing effect on the uterus.
(There is a third option) Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and uterine relaxing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh trifolium pratense flowers and dry them for 24 hours in a single layer.
Crush 15 grams of dried trifolium pratense flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 25 grams of vegetable cellulose powder and 10 grams of silica in a bowl.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the powder mixture.
Label the capsules with the date of production and dosage information and store them in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Raspberry capsules helps with cramps because they contain potent antioxidants, flavonoids, and fragarine, which have a natural ability to relax and soothe muscle contractions. The herbal extract has been traditionally used to alleviate menstrual cramp discomfort, reducing the severity and duration of painful episodes. Additionally, raspberry capsules may help regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to cramping. remedy_properties: Antispasmodic effects: Raspberry leaves contain compounds like anthocyanins, flavonoids, and tannins that relax smooth muscle tissue, reducing cramp severity and frequency by calming spasms in the uterus, intestines, and other areas prone to cramping.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and polyphenols present in raspberry capsules can help reduce inflammation in the body, which may contribute to cramps by alleviating underlying conditions such as endometriosis or irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

Estrogen-regulating effects: Raspberry leaves contain compounds that have been shown to influence estrogen levels, potentially helping to alleviate cramping associated with hormonal fluctuations during menstruation, pregnancy, or menopause.
remedy_constituents: Salicin: Salicin, a phenolic compound, helps alleviate cramps by reducing inflammation and acting as a mild analgesic to ease pain.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate cramps and other menstrual-related issues.

Fruits: Although the fruits themselves are edible, they contain compounds that may help ease menstrual cramps, which are often used in herbal remedies.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh rubus idaeus leaves and flowers at dawn to ensure peak potency and flavor.
Dry the harvested rubus idaeus in a low-temperature dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried rubus idaeus into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 2 teaspoons of the rubus idaeus powder with 1 teaspoon of gelatin in a small bowl for 10 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve the herbal remedy for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black cohosh capsules helps with cramps because they contain a unique combination of compounds that work together to relax muscle tissues and reduce inflammation. The plant's active ingredients, such as cimiragenin and cimicifugin, have been shown to bind to receptors in the body, blocking pain signals and calming muscle spasms. Additionally, black cohosh's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and ease cramping discomfort, providing fast and effective relief from menstrual cramps and other types of musculoskeletal pain.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Black cohosh contains isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce uterine spasms and relax smooth muscle tissue, helping to alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms.
Friedelin: Friedelin, a triterpene found in black cohosh, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with menstruation, including cramps.
Actein: Actein is another key compound in black cohosh that has been found to have anti-spasmodic effects, which can help relieve muscle spasms and cramping during menstruation.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic glycosides (specifically, actein and cimicifugic acid): These compounds have anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to relax smooth muscles and reduce cramping.
Isoferulic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic effects, which help to alleviate menstrual cramps by reducing inflammation and relaxing uterine muscles.

Alkaloids (specifically, berberine): Although berberine is more commonly associated with other plants like Berberis and Hydrastis, it has been found to be present in small amounts in Actaea racemosa. Berberine has anti-inflammatory and anti-spasmodic properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used to make capsules for cramps because they contain actein, a compound that helps to relax uterine muscles.
Barks: Used to make capsules for cramps because they contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles.
Roots: Used to make capsules for cramps because they also contain other compounds like isocryptomerin and acteoside that have anti-spasmodic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest actaea racemosa roots in late summer or early fall after the plant has gone to seed.
Dry the roots in a warm dry place for at least two weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or cellulose in a 1:2 ratio.
Fill empty capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with cramps because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and ease discomfort. The herb's natural diuretic properties also enable the body to release excess fluids and toxins, further alleviating cramp symptoms. Additionally, dandelion's magnesium content can help regulate electrolyte balance and relax tense muscles, providing quick relief from cramps. Overall, herbal dandelion capsules offer a safe and effective way to alleviate menstrual cramps and promote overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Diuretic effects: Dandelion's diuretic properties help to reduce water retention and bloating, which can contribute to menstrual cramp relief by reducing pressure on the uterus.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol and taraxerin, which may help to reduce prostaglandins (hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions) and alleviate menstrual cramp pain.

Vasodilatory effects: Dandelion's vasodilatory properties can help to relax blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing constrictive pressure on the uterus, which may help to ease menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: Helps alleviate cramps by acting as a spasmolytic agent, reducing muscle contractions and promoting relaxation.
Taraxasterol acetate: Contributes to cramp relief by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help soothe and calm the affected area.
Flavonoids (specifically, apigenin): Plays a crucial role in cramp relief by exhibiting antispasmodic properties, which help regulate muscle contractions and promote smooth muscle relaxation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain taraxasterol, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate cramps.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale contain taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, which have been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, thus providing relief from cramps.
Seeds: The seeds of Taraxacum officinale are a rich source of saponins, which are believed to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing cramp pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 30 grams of taraxacum officinale roots and leaves from a clean and dry environment.
Dry the collected plant material for 2 hours at 50 degrees celsius in a food dehydrator.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 250 milligrams of the taraxacum officinale powder with a suitable capsule filler material in a small bowl.
Fill 10 empty gelatin capsules with the taraxacum officinale mixture using a small spoon and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with cramps because it contains a natural compound called isovaleric acid, which has been shown to relax muscle tissue and reduce spasms. This can be especially helpful for women experiencing menstrual cramps, as valerian has been found to reduce the intensity of these symptoms by increasing blood flow to the uterus and relaxing uterine muscles. remedy_properties: Muscle Relaxant Properties: Valerian root contains compounds like isovaleric acid and actinidine that have been shown to relax muscle spasms, reducing the severity and frequency of menstrual cramps.
Pain Relief Compounds: Valerian root contains analgesic compounds like sesquiterpenes that can help reduce pain perception, providing relief from cramping and discomfort associated with menstruation.

Sedative and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The sedative properties of valerian can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can exacerbate menstrual cramps, while its anti-inflammatory effects may help reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and pain.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and chop them into small pieces weighing approximately 200 grams.
Dry the chopped valeriana officinalis roots in a warm dry place for seven days or until crispy and brittle.
Grind dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part valeriana officinalis powder with two parts cornstarch or silica gel and fill empty #0 capsules weighing approximately 500 milligrams.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container for up to six months or until needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White willow capsules helps with cramps because they contain salicin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, salicin works to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine muscle contractions and lead to cramping. By inhibiting these prostaglandins, white willow capsules help to alleviate menstrual cramps, making them a popular natural remedy for women experiencing dysmenorrhea.
remedy_properties: Salicin: Salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, is responsible for the anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects of white willow, helping to reduce cramp intensity and alleviate discomfort.
Fenolic Acids: Fenolic acids in white willow have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms, providing relief from cramps and menstrual cramps.
Tannins: Tannins in white willow have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with cramps, making them a valuable component of the capsules.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the white willow plant contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with menstrual cramps.
Barks: The bark of the white willow plant contains salicin and other compounds that help to relax muscles and reduce spasms, making it effective in relieving menstrual cramp discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh willow bark salix alba leaves from sustainable sources in july or august when essential oil content peaks.
Dry the harvested willow bark leaves using air circulation for 7-10 days or sun drying for 3-5 days.
Crush the dried willow bark leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to ensure uniform consistency.
Mix 500mg of crushed willow bark powder with 250mg of organic rice flour to create a blend and fill 100 capsules.
Store the filled herbal capsules in an airtight container protected from light and moisture for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blue cohosh capsules helps with cramps because of its ability to relax uterine muscles, reducing spasms and contractions. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh caulophyllum thalictroides roots in the spring or summer for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested roots thoroughly using a food dehydrator at 50°c for 24 hours.
Chop the dried root material into small pieces and combine with 400mg of silica gel beads in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks to allow for transference of active compounds.
Fill empty capsules with 500mg of the prepared herbal mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency and shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with cramps because they contain mucilage, a natural substance that soothes and protects the lining of the digestive tract. Prebiotic fiber: Slippery elm capsules contain prebiotic fiber that helps feed good bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy gut microbiome and reducing symptoms of digestive discomfort, including cramping.

Barks: The inner bark of slippery elm has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce muscle spasms and ease the discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra bark by cutting it from mature trees during the spring or autumn season.
Dry the harvested bark in a warm, well-ventilated area for several days or use a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Grind the dried ulmus rubra bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder for about 10 minutes.
Mix one part of the powdered ulmus rubra with two parts of gelatin capsules in a small bowl and fill the capsules by spooning the mixture into each capsule shell.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or freeze them for up to a year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with cramps because of its natural ability to relax muscles and ease spasms. The herb's bioactive compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, work together to calm down the nervous system, reducing muscle contractions that cause cramps. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with cramping. By taking herbal lemon balm capsules, individuals can experience relief from menstrual cramps, stomach cramps, and other types of muscular spasms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm capsules have antispasmodic effects, which help to relax muscle spasms and relieve cramp pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected area, further reducing cramp severity and discomfort.
GABA-enhancing properties: Lemon balm contains GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate muscle tone and relax muscles, which can help alleviate menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, roots help to relax muscles and reduce spasms associated with menstrual cramps.
Buds: Containing high amounts of citral, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, buds help to alleviate cramping pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves measure out 30 grams of dried leaves.
Dry the leaves using air circulation at room temperature for 24 hours store in an airtight container.
Grind the dried melissa officinalis leaves into fine powder using a spice grinder mix with 100 milligrams of ginger powder and 50 milligrams of cramp bark powder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the herbal blend fill to top using a capsule filler.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with cramps because of its natural ability to relax and soothe muscle contractions. The herb contains a compound called chamazulene, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and spasms in the muscles. Additionally, yarrow's flavonoids and saponins work together to calm the nervous system, reducing tension and promoting relaxation of the muscles. As a result, herbal yarrow capsules provide natural relief from menstrual cramps, helping women manage their symptoms and regain comfort during this time of the month.
remedy_properties: Spasmolytic properties: Yarrow capsules contain flavonoids that help relax smooth muscle tissue, reducing muscle spasms and cramping.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones, help reduce inflammation in the affected area, which can contribute to cramp relief.
Pain-relieving properties: The volatile oils in yarrow capsules have analgesic and anesthetic effects, helping to numb or relieve pain caused by cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to relax uterine muscles and relieve menstrual cramp symptoms.
Seeds: Yarrow seeds contain a high concentration of alkaloids that help to reduce inflammation and ease cramping associated with menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated carefully.
Clean and dry the harvested achillea millefolium leaves and flowers thoroughly within 24 hours of harvest.
Combine one part dried achillea millefolium with two parts carrier herb such as corn silk or chamomile in a bowl finely.
Steep the herbal mixture in boiling water for 10-15 minutes then strain and discard solids carefully.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled herbal liquid to a dose of 300mg per capsule immediately before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsules helps with cramps because of its ability to relax muscles and reduce inflammation. The herb's primary active compound, parthenolide, has been shown to inhibit the release of serotonin, a neurotransmitter that can cause muscle spasms. Additionally, feverfew's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and ease pain associated with menstrual cramps. By taking feverfew capsules, women may experience relief from severe cramping, making them an effective natural remedy for managing menstrual discomfort.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew's primary bioactive compound, parthenolide, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help relieve muscle cramps by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles.

Feverfew extracts: The extract of feverfew herb contains flavonoids, terpenes, and sesquiterpenes that have analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and calcium channel-blocking properties, which can help alleviate muscle spasms and reduce the frequency and severity of cramps.

Volatile oils: Feverfew's volatile oils, such as chamazulene and borneol, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe muscle pain and reduce inflammation associated with cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of feverfew contain parthenolide, a sesquiterpene lactone that helps to alleviate menstrual cramps and spasms.
Flowers: Feverfew flowers are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce cramping and discomfort during menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers from a trusted source or purchase them online.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 500 milligrams of the tanacetum parthenium powder with 250 milligrams of excipients such as silica or calcium carbonate in a small bowl.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency, taking approximately 5 minutes per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or freeze for longer preservation, allowing for immediate use when needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Motherwort capsules helps with cramps because it contains natural compounds that work to relax uterine muscles, reducing spasms and alleviating menstrual pain. The herb's vasodilatory properties also help to improve blood flow, which can ease cramping by delivering more oxygen and nutrients to the uterus. Additionally, motherwort's anti-inflammatory effects may reduce inflammation in the pelvic area, further contributing to its ability to provide relief from cramps. By addressing these underlying causes, motherwort capsules offer a natural solution for women seeking to manage menstrual discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Motherwort contains flavonoids and diterpenoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce uterine contractions and alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Antispasmodic properties: The iridoid glycosides present in Motherwort capsules relax the uterine muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from menstrual cramps and discomfort.
Stress-relieving and sedative properties: Motherwort's sesquiterpenes have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress that often accompanies menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: Motherwort roots are valued for their ability to relax uterine muscles and reduce menstrual cramp severity.
Seeds: Motherwort seeds contain compounds that help to alleviate spasms and cramping in the uterus, making them effective in alleviating menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leonurus cardiaca leaves and flowers of high quality ensuring 100g for this recipe.
Dry the harvested material using shade drying method or air circulation dryer at low temperature.
Grind dried leonurus cardiaca into fine powder weighing 50g for future use.
Mix the powdered leonurus cardiaca with 150ml of water and let it steep for 1 hour to create infusion.
Fill 100-capsule size gelatin capsules with 250mg each of the prepared herbal infusion and store in airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stiff trillium capsules helps with cramps because of its natural ability to relax muscles and ease spasms. The capsules contain a potent extract from the Trillium erectum plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to soothe menstrual cramps, backaches, and other types of muscle spasms. By calming irritated nerves and relaxing tense muscles, stiff trillium capsules provide fast and effective relief from cramping pain, allowing women to go about their daily activities without discomfort or interruption.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Trillium erectum contains compounds that relax smooth muscle tissue, which helps to alleviate spasms and cramps in the body by reducing muscle contractions.

Pain-relieving: The plant's active compounds, such as trillin and trilobine, have analgesic properties, which help to numb or reduce pain caused by menstrual cramps, headaches, and other types of cramping.
Anti-inflammatory: Trillium erectum has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to muscle cramps and spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in triterpenoids and alkaloids, which provide anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Rhizomes: Containing glycosides and saponins, these underground stems are used to treat abdominal cramps, spasms, and other symptoms associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried trillium erectum roots of specific species in late spring.
Clean and dry the roots by gently brushing off dirt particles and excess moisture.
Cut the dried roots into small pieces and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered root with 200 milliliters of vegetable glycerin in a small bowl and let it steep for 2 hours at room temperature.
Fill empty capsules with 250-milligram portions of the trillium erectum extract mixture and seal them tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage capsules helps with cramps because they contain gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), an omega-6 fatty acid that has anti-inflammatory properties. GLA works by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscle contractions, which can help alleviate menstrual cramps caused by uterine contractions. Additionally, borage oil's GLA has been shown to increase prostaglandins, hormone-like compounds that help relax the uterine muscles, further reducing cramp severity. By promoting a healthy balance of these hormones, herbal borage capsules provide natural relief from menstrual cramps and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Gamma-linolenic acid (GLA): Helps reduce inflammation and relax muscle spasms, which can alleviate menstrual cramp pain.
Omega-6 fatty acids: Supports the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that help regulate uterine contractions and reduce cramping.
Bromelain: Has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce menstrual cramp severity by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the uterus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, borage roots help to relax muscle contractions and ease menstrual cramp pain.
Seeds: Packed with omega-6 fatty acids, borage seeds reduce inflammation and promote hormonal balance, alleviating menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis leaves and flowers then dry them in shade for 2-3 weeks.
Grind dried plant material into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Sift powder through mesh strainer to remove any lumps or large particles then mix with silica gel.
Fill empty capsules with approximately 250mg of powdered borago officinalis mixture then seal tightly.
Store prepared capsules in airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for cramps are a natural supplement that helps alleviate menstrual cramp symptoms by reducing pain and inflammation. The best herbal capsules for cramps include Paeonia lactiflora, Cimicifuga racemosa, Zingiber officinale, Angelica sinensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Vitex agnus-castus, Ginkgo biloba, Angelica archangelica, Foeniculum vulgare, and Dioscorea villosa. If left untreated, cramps can disrupt daily life, causing missed work, social events, and emotional distress, ultimately affecting overall well-being and relationships.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to cramps that are treated with herbal capsules are spasms, muscle contractions, and menstrual disorders. Herbal capsules like Valerian root, Passionflower, and Dong quai have been used to alleviate symptoms such as muscle stiffness, twitching, and pain. These natural remedies can provide relief from cramp-like conditions, including menstrual cramps, fibromyalgia, and sciatica, by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and regulating hormonal balance.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with cramps is a blend of Dong Quai, Cramp Bark, and Ginger. Dong Quai helps to regulate menstrual cycles, while Cramp Bark targets spasms and pain. Ginger, rich in anti-inflammatory properties, aids in reducing inflammation and discomfort. This synergy works together to alleviate cramping, soothe the uterus, and promote overall hormonal balance. Regular consumption of these herbal capsules can lead to a significant reduction in menstrual cramp severity and frequency. Consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of midwifery & women's health, 180 female students aged 18 to 27 with primary dysmenorrhea were randomly divided into three groups: herbal drug, mefenamic acid, and placebo. The herbal drug group was given 500 mg of highly purified saffron, celery seed, and anise (SCA) extracts three times a day for three days, starting from the onset of bleeding or pain. The results showed that the herbal drug group had a significant reduction in pain scores and pain duration scores compared to the placebo group, with a magnitude of reduction significantly greater than the mefenamic acid group, making herbal capsules a good option for relieving cramps.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora capsules helps with cramps because they contain a high concentration of paeoniflorin, a bioactive compound with potent anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties. This herb has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to soothe menstrual cramps and promote relaxation. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the uterine muscles, paeonia lactiflora capsules can provide effective relief from menstrual cramps, allowing individuals to manage their symptoms and improve their overall well-being. Its natural and gentle approach makes it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic solution.
remedy_constituents: Isoferulic acid: It has antispasmodic properties, helping to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms associated with cramps.
Paeoniflorin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by cramps.
Paeonol: It exhibits antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relax muscles, reduce pain, and provide relief from cramp symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain paenol, a compound that helps to relieve menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS.
Rhyzomes: They are rich in glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help to alleviate menstrual cramps and other pain.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to reduce menstrual cramps and other symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of dried paeonia lactiflora flowers and roots from a trusted supplier or your own garden.
Crush the harvested plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 2 grams of the powdered plant material with 2 grams of vegetable cellulose and 1 gram of magnesium stearate.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler for 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa capsules helps with cramps because they contain a natural blend of compounds that target the root cause of menstrual discomfort. The herb, also known as black cohosh, has been traditionally used to ease menstrual cramps, bloating, and other symptoms associated with PMS. By regulating hormone levels and reducing inflammation, Cimicifuga racemosa capsules can provide quick relief from cramps, allowing women to manage their symptoms naturally and effectively. This makes it a popular choice among those seeking a holistic approach to menstrual health.
remedy_constituents: Isoferulic acid: This constituent has anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cramps.
Sesquiterpene glycosides: These compounds have a relaxing effect on the uterine muscles, reducing spasms and alleviating cramping.
Rotundifuran: This lignan has estrogenic activity, helping to regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of actinide and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate menstrual cramps.
Barks: They may also be used, although less commonly, due to their potential to have similar benefits as the roots and rhizomes, although more research is needed to confirm this.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried cimicifuga racemosa root and 10 grams of vegetable capsules in a clean environment.
Grind the cimicifuga racemosa root into a fine powder using a mill or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 10 grams of the fine powder with 1 gram of silica and 1 gram of magnesium stearate.
Fill the vegetable capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine for 5 minutes.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with cramps because they contain the active compound gingerol, a natural anti-inflammatory agent. Gingerol has been found to relax the uterine muscles, reducing spasmodic contractions and alleviating menstrual cramp pain. Additionally, ginger's warming properties help to increase blood flow and reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause cramping. As a result, ginger capsules can provide fast and effective relief from menstrual cramps, making them a popular natural remedy for women experiencing this common symptom.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate cramp pain by reducing inflammation and numbing pain receptors.
Shogaols: These compounds have a similar effect to gingerols, but are more stable and have a longer-lasting effect, contributing to their role in relieving cramp discomfort.
Volatile oils (including zingiberene): These sesquiterpenes have anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties, which help relax muscles and reduce spasms associated with cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used in capsule form for cramps due to their ability to stimulate digestion and relieve nausea.

Leaves: Although less commonly used than rhizomes or roots, ginger leaves are sometimes used in capsule form to help relieve menstrual cramps due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather and clean 2 pounds of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes with no signs of mold or rot.
Dry the rhizomes in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Grind 200 grams of dried zingiber officinale rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 1 gram of powder with 2 grams of cellulose powder to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the blend and seal them tightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis capsules helps with cramps because they contain a unique blend of bioactive compounds, particularly ferulic acid and isoflavones, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These compounds work together to relax the uterine muscles, reducing the severity and frequency of menstrual cramps. Additionally, Angelica sinensis capsules may also help to regulate hormonal imbalances and improve blood flow, further contributing to their cramp-relieving effects, providing natural relief for women experiencing dysmenorrhea.
remedy_constituents: Coumarins: They help with cramps by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which reduce pain and muscle spasms.
Phenolic acids (specifically ferulic acid): They help with cramps by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing prostaglandin production and alleviating pain and discomfort.
Lignans: They help with cramps by exhibiting a sedative effect, which helps to relax muscles and reduce muscle spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica sinensis are the most commonly used part due to their high content of ferulic acid and other bioactive compounds that help relax muscles and reduce pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Angelica sinensis are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce cramping and spasms by inhibiting the release of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
Leaves: The leaves of Angelica sinensis are used for their ability to relax smooth muscle and reduce pain, making them effective in relieving menstrual cramps and other types of cramping.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried angelica sinensis root, 500 grams of gelatin capsules, and a capsule filler.
Grind the dried angelica sinensis root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 2.5 grams of the ground angelica sinensis powder with 2.5 grams of powdered gelatin in a bowl.
Fill 500 gelatin capsules with the mixture using a small spoon or capsule filler for 30 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container to preserve their potency for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with cramps because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, has a soothing effect on the muscles, reducing muscle spasms and cramps. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has a balancing effect on hormonal fluctuations, which can contribute to menstrual cramps. It also helps to relax the uterine muscles, reducing the severity and frequency of cramps. Overall, Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules can provide natural relief from cramps, making it a popular choice for those seeking a herbal solution.
remedy_constituents: Licoric acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce muscle spasms and relieve cramp pain.
Glycyrrhizin: It has a diuretic effect, which helps reduce water retention and alleviate cramping associated with menstrual cycles.
Isoliquiritigenin: It has a relaxing effect on smooth muscle, which helps reduce muscle spasms and relieve cramp pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate cramp symptoms.
Leaves: They are used because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help to relax muscles and reduce cramp frequency.
Rhyzomes: They are used as they have a high glycyrrhizin content, similar to roots, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramp pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Grind the cleaned roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 10g of cellulose powder in a large bowl to create a uniform blend.
Fill a capsule machine with the blended mixture and encapsulate 500mg per capsule for 2 hours.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus capsules helps with cramps because they contain a natural hormone balancer that regulates menstrual cycles and reduces uterine contractions. The capsules work by stimulating the pituitary gland to produce luteinizing hormone, which helps to regulate ovulation and menstrual flow. This can lead to a decrease in the severity and frequency of cramps. Additionally, Vitex agnus-castus has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe and calm the uterine muscles, providing relief from cramps and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Iridoids help with cramps by exerting anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which reduce muscle contractions and alleviate discomfort.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids help with cramps by acting as natural anti-spasmodics, which help to relax muscles and reduce menstrual cramp severity.
Phenylethanoid glycosides: Phenylethanoid glycosides help with cramps by inhibiting prostaglandin synthesis, which contributes to reduced uterine contractions and alleviated menstrual cramp symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to make capsules for cramps due to their high concentration of agnuside and flavonoids, which are believed to have a regulatory effect on the menstrual cycle.
Seeds: They are used to make capsules for cramps as they are thought to have a sedative effect on the uterus, helping to alleviate menstrual cramps.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules for cramps due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that may help to regulate hormonal balance and reduce menstrual cramp severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried vitex agnus-castus berries from a reputable supplier or your own garden.
Grind the dried berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle within 10 minutes.
Mix 1 part of the powdered vitex agnus-castus with 2 parts of a carrier herb such as cornstarch within 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a capsule filler machine within 15 minutes.
Label and date the capsules and store them in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with cramps because they improve blood flow to the muscles, reducing inflammation and spasms. The antioxidants in Ginkgo biloba also help to relax the uterine muscles, relieving cramping and discomfort. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe cramps and promote overall well-being. As a result, many women find relief from menstrual cramps and other types of muscle cramps when taking Ginkgo biloba capsules. This natural remedy can provide much-needed relief.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps to relax smooth muscle and reduce muscle spasms, which can alleviate cramp symptoms.
Flavonoids (specifically quercetin): Have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce muscle inflammation and oxidative stress associated with cramps.
Ginkgolides (specifically ginkgolide B): Act as vasodilators, which can help improve blood flow and reduce muscle spasms, potentially alleviating cramp symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of Ginkgo biloba extract, containing flavonoids and terpenoids that help alleviate cramps.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds contain a compound called ginkgotoxin, which, although toxic in large amounts, has been used in small quantities to relieve menstrual cramps.
Buds: The buds of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help soothe and relax the uterine muscles, reducing menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 ginkgo biloba leaves from a healthy plant in the morning when the moisture content is highest.
Dry the leaves in a dehydrator at 80 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours to reduce moisture content.
Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at a low speed setting.
Mix 1 gram of the powdered ginkgo biloba with a binding agent such as gelatin or starch in a ratio of 1:1.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica capsules helps with cramps because they contain compounds like ferulic acid and angelic acid, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce muscle spasms and relax the uterine muscles, providing relief from menstrual cramps. Additionally, the capsule's anti-spasmodic properties help to calm the digestive system, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with IBS and other gastrointestinal issues. This natural remedy can provide a safe and effective solution for individuals seeking relief from cramps.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic glycosides: They help with cramps by reducing inflammation and relaxing the smooth muscles in the uterus and other affected areas.
Terpenoids: They help with cramps by reducing spasms and pain through their sedative and antispasmodic properties.
Coumarins: They help with cramps by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation, which helps to relieve cramp pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain high levels of ferulic acid, which helps relax the muscles and relieve cramps.
Leaves: They have a rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, reducing cramp severity.
Stems: They contain a compound called angelic acid, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from cramps and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves from a clean environment after 30 minutes of morning dew collection.
Clean the harvested materials with filtered water and let them dry in a well-ventilated area for 24 hours.
Grind 2 grams of dried angelica archangelica roots and leaves into a fine powder using a grinder.
Mix the powder with 1 gram of gelatin in a small bowl and add a few drops of purified water to create a paste.
Fill gelatin capsules with the paste using a capsule filler and seal them immediately to maintain potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare capsules helps with cramps because they contain the active compound anethole, which has a natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effect. This means that it can help to relax the muscles in the uterus, reducing the severity and frequency of menstrual cramps. Additionally, the capsule's soothing properties can also help to ease digestive discomfort, which is often associated with cramps. Overall, Foeniculum vulgare capsules offer a natural and effective way to manage menstrual cramps.
remedy_constituents: Ferulic acid: It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce muscle spasms and ease cramp pain.
Anethole: It has a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles, which helps to reduce muscle cramps and spasms, particularly in the digestive tract.
Trans-anethole: It acts as a smooth muscle relaxant, which helps to ease muscle cramps and spasms, and also has a mild sedative effect to promote relaxation.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Foeniculum vulgare are commonly used to make capsules for cramps due to their high carminative and antispasmodic properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Foeniculum vulgare are often used to make capsules for cramps because they contain compounds that help relax the uterine muscles and ease menstrual cramps.
Stems: The stems of Foeniculum vulgare are sometimes used to make capsules for cramps due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relax the smooth muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried foeniculum vulgare flowers and leaves in sufficient quantities for production of herbal capsules.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare further in a low temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a mill or grinder.
Mix the foeniculum vulgare powder with a suitable capsule filler such as potato starch in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the foeniculum vulgare powder mixture using a manual capsule filler machine.

remedies.remedy_desc: Dioscorea villosa capsules helps with cramps because they are derived from the wild yam root, a natural anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving agent. The root contains diosgenin, a compound that has been shown to relax the uterine muscles and reduce spasms, providing relief from menstrual cramps. Additionally, Dioscorea villosa capsules have been used for centuries in traditional medicine to alleviate discomfort and promote relaxation, making them a popular natural remedy for cramp relief. This natural approach can be especially beneficial for those seeking alternative solutions to manage their cramp symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Diosgenin: Diosgenin helps alleviate cramps by inhibiting the contractions of smooth muscles and reducing the release of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that can stimulate uterine contractions.
Saponins: Saponins in Dioscorea villosa help reduce cramps by stabilizing the membranes of smooth muscle cells, thereby preventing the excessive contractions that lead to cramping.
Steroidal sapogenins: Steroidal sapogenins help reduce cramps by exerting a relaxing effect on the uterine muscle and reducing the severity of menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are commonly used due to their high concentration of diosgenin, a steroidal saponin that helps to reduce menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes are also used for their diosgenin content, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and uterine-relaxing properties that help to alleviate menstrual cramps.
Barks: The barks are used in some herbal supplements due to their potential diosgenin content, which may help to reduce menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dioscorea villosa root in late summer or early fall after the plant has matured.
Wash the harvested root thoroughly with water to remove dirt and debris then pat dry with paper towels.
Cut the cleaned dioscorea villosa root into small pieces and dry them in a dehydrator at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried dioscorea villosa root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder at 1000 rpm for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the dioscorea villosa powder and seal them tightly using a capsule sealer.
